title: Straty ludzkie i materialne w Powstaniu Warszawskim
geographic_scope: Warsaw; distinguishes left-bank Warsaw from Praga
strengths: Conflict-specific synthesis that explicitly separates left-bank Warsaw, reports multiple literature clusters for civilian deaths, and provides insurgent and some German casualty figures.
limitations: Not a microdata registry; some military-loss figures, especially German losses, reproduce disputed legacy estimates.
notes: Used as the principal open-access denominator and civilian-loss anchor, but German-loss figures are cross-checked against IPN.

title: Warsaw Uprising
pages_or_sections: Sections 'The Uprising, August 1–October 2, 1944', 'Civilians during the Uprising', and 'Jews and the Warsaw Uprising'
strengths: Clear English synthesis with explicit side attribution for civilian massacres and useful statements on combatant-status ambiguity among Jews hiding in Warsaw.
limitations: High-level encyclopedia entry without a transparent casualty methodology appendix.
notes: Especially useful for side attribution and episode chronology.

title: Warsaw Rising and Warsaw Ghetto Uprising (ENG infographic)
pages_or_sections: Course of events / fighters engaged / casualties and losses panels
strengths: Excellent for episodeization and a rough upper military-exposure bound via the 'c. 50,000' per side graphic.
limitations: Teaching infographic with sparse source citation; treated as corroborative rather than decisive.
notes: Used mainly to anchor the proposed sub-episode split and the high military-person-month scenario.

title: Dulag 121 – the Gate into the Unknown. Dramatic mass expulsions during the Warsaw Rising
strengths: Directly relevant to civilian exposure reduction and displacement timing.
limitations: Museum narrative, not a formal demographic reconstruction; detained totals are themselves reported as a wide range.
notes: Used to justify declining civilian exposure over the course of the episode.

title: Maria Tarnowska: The Willful Countess
pages_or_sections: September evacuation passages and the Borkiewicz-cited October expulsions
strengths: Provides a rare open-access anchor for how many civilians were still present late in the battle.
limitations: Biographical article; the expulsion totals are quoted from Borkiewicz and German sources rather than documented in full within the article.
notes: Used to anchor the end-of-episode civilian stock at roughly 155,519 to 200,000 civilians.

title: Straty ludzkie w Powstaniu Warszawskim
pages_or_sections: Sections on overall losses, expulsions, and military losses
strengths: Explicitly explains why German casualty figures differ and preserves both low incomplete-report figures and higher legacy estimates.
limitations: Synthesis article, not the underlying archival or registry publication.
notes: Used to set the best German KIA value at ~3,000 and the lower bound at 2,000; also supports the >63,000 noncombat civilian lower bound.
